plant_side_effects.name: induces allergic reactions
plant_side_effects.plant_side_effect_desc: Chamomilla recutita induces allergic reactions in some individuals, often causing symptoms such as skin rashes, itching, and hives. This is due to the plant's high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, which can trigger an immune response in people sensitive to them. As a result, individuals experiencing allergic reactions to chamomilla recutita may need to avoid using products containing the plant or take precautions when handling it.

plant_side_effects.name: triggers diarrhea
plant_side_effects.plant_side_effect_desc: Chamomilla recutita triggers diarrhea in some individuals, which can be a concern for those who are prone to gastrointestinal issues. This side effect is often associated with high doses or prolonged use of the plant, and can be a result of its ability to stimulate the digestive system. In some cases, the diarrhea may be accompanied by other symptoms, such as stomach cramps or nausea.

plant_side_effects.name: causes gastrointestinal issues
plant_side_effects.plant_side_effect_desc: Chamomilla recutita causes gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, as a result of its active compounds interacting with the digestive system. These gastrointestinal symptoms can be particularly problematic for individuals who consume large quantities of the plant or use it in conjunction with other medications. In severe cases, gastrointestinal issues related to chamomilla recutita consumption can lead to dehydration and electrolyte imbalances.

plant_side_effects.name: induces nausea
plant_side_effects.plant_side_effect_desc: Chamomilla recutita induces nausea in some individuals due to its potential to cause stomach upset and gastrointestinal irritation. This side effect is often associated with consuming large amounts of chamomile tea or supplements. However, it's essential to note that the severity of nausea can vary greatly from person to person.

plant_side_effects.name: causes anaphylaxis
plant_side_effects.plant_side_effect_desc: N/A

plant_side_effects.name: produces convulsions
plant_side_effects.plant_side_effect_desc: Chamomilla recutita produces convulsions in some individuals due to its potential neurotoxic effects, particularly when consumed in large quantities or in cases of prolonged use. This can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to have neuroexcitatory properties. However, such adverse reactions are relatively rare and mostly occur in people with pre-existing health conditions or those who are sensitive to the plant's active compounds.

plant_side_effects.name: induces headache
plant_side_effects.plant_side_effect_desc: Chamomilla recutita induces headache due to its ability to stimulate the nervous system and cause an imbalance in brain chemistry. This imbalance can lead to tension headaches, which are often described as a dull, throbbing pain on one or both sides of the head. In some individuals, chamomilla recutita consumption may exacerbate existing headache conditions, such as migraines or cluster headaches.

plant_side_effects.name: produces respiratory depression
plant_side_effects.plant_side_effect_desc: Chamomilla recutita produces respiratory depression, which is a potentially serious side effect that can occur when consuming large amounts of the plant's essential oil. This condition can lead to a decrease in breathing rate and may cause dizziness, confusion, and even loss of consciousness. In severe cases, respiratory depression can be life-threatening and requires immediate medical attention.

plant_side_effects.name: causes bradycardia
plant_side_effects.plant_side_effect_desc: Chamomilla recutita causes bradycardia, a condition characterized by a slower than normal heart rate, as a potential side effect of its consumption. This is due to the plant's ability to affect the body's autonomic nervous system, leading to a reduction in heart rate. However, it is essential to consult a medical professional before using chamomilla recutita, especially for individuals with pre-existing heart conditions.

plant_side_effects.name: triggers fatigue
plant_side_effects.plant_side_effect_desc: Chamomilla recutita triggers fatigue in some individuals, potentially due to its sedative properties and ability to slow down the central nervous system. This side effect can vary in severity and may be more pronounced in people who are sensitive to the plant's active compounds or those who consume large amounts of chamomile products. As a result, individuals should be aware of their body's response to chamomile and adjust their consumption accordingly to avoid excessive drowsiness or fatigue.

plant_side_effects.name: triggers asthma attacks
plant_side_effects.plant_side_effect_desc: Chamomilla recutita triggers asthma attacks due to the presence of allergenic compounds such as apigenin and chamazulene, which can cause respiratory irritation and exacerbate asthma symptoms. These compounds can also trigger allergic reactions in individuals who are sensitive to ragweed and other plants in the same family. As a result, people with asthma should exercise caution when using chamomilla recutita products, such as tea or supplements, and consult with a healthcare professional before consuming them.

plant_side_effects.name: causes dyspepsia
plant_side_effects.plant_side_effect_desc: Chamomilla recutita causes dyspepsia, a common side effect associated with the consumption of this plant. This gastrointestinal issue is often characterized by symptoms such as bloating, abdominal pain, and discomfort. In some cases, the consumption of chamomilla recutita may exacerbate existing digestive problems, making it essential to consult a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment.

plant_side_effects.name: produces hormonal imbalance
plant_side_effects.plant_side_effect_desc: N/A

plant_side_effects.name: interferes with blood clotting
plant_side_effects.plant_side_effect_desc: Chamomilla recutita interferes with blood clotting, which can lead to increased risk of bruising and bleeding. This effect is thought to be due to the plant's ability to inhibit the production of certain clotting factors in the body. As a result, individuals taking chamomilla recutita may need to be cautious when engaging in activities that pose a risk of injury or trauma.

plant_side_effects.name: induces eczema
plant_side_effects.plant_side_effect_desc: N/A

plant_side_effects.name: triggers insomnia
plant_side_effects.plant_side_effect_desc: Chamomilla recutita triggers insomnia in some individuals due to its sedative properties, which can lead to difficulty falling asleep or staying asleep throughout the night. This is often a result of the plant's ability to affect the body's natural sleep-wake cycle, causing it to become disrupted. As a result, people taking chamomilla recutita may need to adjust their dosage or consider alternative treatments to manage their insomnia effectively.
